meta.reportLabel: Thu - BOARD CONSOLIDATES: BoC held 2.25% (6th straight) with an oil-conditional hawkish tilt (Macklem: hikes possible IF oil spikes; growth resumed). The soft-USD leg kept working (DXY 2nd down day <101, ~100.8) but the oil & gold legs COOLED - WTI stalled ~$79.6 despite fresh US strikes, gold gave back to ~$4,058, silver -0.6%. Short USD/CAD stays the cleanest trade but is now one-and-a-half legs (oil stopped adding). USOIL & XAU downgrade to RANGE. All oil-linked hostage to Sat Jul 18 Oman talks.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: A day of consolidation, not a new regime: the soft-USD leg kept working while the oil and gold legs cooled. The BoC held 2.25% (6th straight) with an explicitly oil-conditional hawkish hold - Macklem said inflation eases if oil declines but hikes are still possible if oil spikes, and growth 'looks to have resumed' (0.7% GDP 2026). CAD stays strongest, but the petro-bid it rode is consolidating: WTI slipped ~$79.6, Brent ~$85, roughly flat-to-lower DESPITE fresh US strikes and the reinstated blockade - the market now caps the Hormuz premium. Gold gave a little back (~$4,058) and silver slipped -0.6%. The one leg still firing is the dollar: DXY logged a 2nd straight down day below 101 (~100.8) as the soft-CPI repricing (July-hike odds ~12%) held. Net: short USD/CAD is still the cleanest trade but now one-and-a-half legs; long USOIL & long gold downgrade to consolidation/range into the Sat Jul 18 Oman talks.
meta.nextBigEvent: Sat Jul 18 Iran-Oman Hormuz talks are the imminent off-ramp - a maritime-security/transit-fee deal crushes the oil/CAD/gold premium in minutes, a breakdown re-spikes it. Then Mon Jul 21 NZ Q2 CPI (ANZ 4.4% / BNZ 4.5%, above RBNZ's 3.9% call) is the NZD swing, and Jul 23 ECB / Jul 28-29 FOMC frame the back half of the month.

dailyRead: A day of consolidation, not a new regime: the soft-USD leg kept working while the oil and gold legs cooled. The BoC held at 2.25% (6th straight) and Macklem gave the trade its cleanest steer - inflation is poised to ease provided oil declines, but hikes are still possible if oil spikes - an explicitly oil-conditional hawkish hold, paired with growth 'looks to have resumed' (BoC now sees 0.7% GDP in 2026, 1.8% in 2027-28). CAD stays the strongest currency, but the petro-bid it rode is consolidating: WTI slipped to ~$79.6 and Brent to ~$85 (roughly flat-to-lower) despite fresh US strikes and the reinstated blockade - the market now fades additional escalation as spare capacity, demand fear and live Oman mediation cap the top. Gold gave a little back too (~$4,058, easing toward $4,000 after yesterday's +2.2% pop), and silver slipped -0.6%. The one leg still firing is the dollar: DXY logged a second straight down day below 101 (~100.8) as the soft-CPI repricing (July-hike odds ~12%) held, EUR/USD sat ~1.14, USD/JPY stayed weak near ~161-162. Net: short USD/CAD is still the cleanest trade - the USD-down leg is intact and BoC handed CAD an oil-conditional hawkish tilt - but it is now a one-and-a-half-leg trade, because oil stopped adding. Long USOIL and long gold both downgrade to consolidation/range into the Saturday Oman talks. Cleanest leans: short USD/CAD, then the soft-USD relief crosses - all still hostage to the Saturday Oman off-ramp.

today.headline: BoC held 2.25% (6th) with an oil-conditional hawkish tilt - Macklem: hikes still possible IF oil spikes; growth resumed
today.read: Still the strongest currency, but the petro-bid is consolidating (WTI ~$79.6 stopped adding). The soft-USD leg keeps USD/CAD pinned toward ~1.40, so USD/CAD SHORT (HIGH) stays the cleanest trade - now one-and-a-half legs. CAD/JPY LONG (MED). Risk: a Sat Oman deal crushes oil AND flips the BoC dovish, unwinding the CAD bid faster than the others.

today.headline: DXY logs a 2nd straight down day below 101 (~100.8) - the soft-CPI repricing (hike odds ~12%) held
today.read: The dollar is the one leg still firing lower, keeping short USD/CAD and the soft-USD relief crosses alive. It's a dovish lean, not a rout, into the Jul 28-29 FOMC (still a hold). Risk: hot July oil-inflation data re-fires the no-cut trade; a fresh escalation haven bid firms DXY.

today.headline: Swing catalyst confirmed HOT - ANZ now 4.4% / BNZ 4.5% for Q2 CPI (Jul 21), ABOVE RBNZ's 3.9% call
today.read: 1-yr inflation expectations jumped to 3.41%; a hot Q2 CPI Jul 21 re-arms the hawks and challenges the dovish-hike. NZD/USD & NZD/JPY tilt up; NZD/CAD short holds on the oil leg. Risk: an oil risk-off leg still hits high-beta NZD; a sub-4% print validates the dovish-hike.

today.headline: No change - oil bill eases a touch, USD/JPY ~161-162; BoJ Jul 31 the next event
today.read: Still the biggest oil-shock loser, but the easing oil price + lower US real rates trim the carry gap. RANGE on USD/JPY; CAD/JPY LONG holds on the oil leg. Risk: oil re-accelerates toward 163/the MoF zone; a panic snaps the carry trade.

today.headline: No change - soft-USD relief holds EUR/USD ~1.14; cooling oil eases the drag
today.read: The softer dollar and the oil pullback relieve the energy-importer laggard at the margin, but not enough for a clean long - RANGE with a mild upward tilt. Jul 23 ECB is the next test. Risk: a firm-USD bounce or oil re-spike re-pins it toward 1.14.

today.headline: No change - soft-USD relief vs oil-risk cap; Jun jobs now due ~Jul 23
today.read: Sticky-core floor and a softer USD help at the margin, but the oil risk-off leg caps it; unemployment ~4.3% loosening. MIXED. Risk: a re-escalation leg re-softens it; a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) + an Oman deal re-arm the hawks.

today.headline: No change - firm rate story with a soft-USD tailwind; UK June CPI ~Jul 22
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, and the softer dollar hands cable a tailwind, though risk-off keeps it a high-beta passenger. GBP/USD RANGE->long tilt. Risk: a risk-off flush; a firm-USD bounce; a soft UK CPI (~Jul 22).

strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (gasoline-driven; ex-gas 2.2%, core ~2.0%); Jun jobs +18.2k beat (unemp 6.5%); BoC held 2.25% (6th straight) - Macklem: hikes still possible IF oil spikes, inflation eases if oil declines, growth 'looks to have resumed' (0.7% GDP 2026). The petro-bid is now consolidating (WTI ~$79.6 stopped adding), but the soft USD keeps USD/CAD pinned toward ~1.40.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ The oil-conditional hawkish BoC stacks on a softer USD to keep USD/CAD near ~1.40; but oil stopped adding, so it's one-and-a-half legs. Risk: a Sat Oman deal crushes oil AND flips BoC dovish; a risk-off flush hits CAD's risk leg. Next BoC Sep (data-dependent).

strength.tag: services CPI 3.7%; softer USD lifts cable
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes). The softer dollar hands cable a tailwind, though risk-off keeps GBP a high-beta passenger.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into Jul 30; the soft USD helps near-term. Risk: a risk-off flush hits it; UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the domestic check; a firm-USD bounce. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.verdict: SOFTER — repricing holds; DXY 2nd down day
strength.drivers: June CPI -0.4% m/m (biggest drop since Apr 2020) -> 3.5% y/y (from 4.2%); core FLAT m/m -> 2.6% (from 2.9%). NFP +57k (Jun, big miss; unemp 4.2%). Fed 3.50-3.75% hold. July-hike odds ~12%; DXY logged a second straight down day below 101 (~100.8) as the repricing held.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ The disinflation repricing stuck (DXY 2nd down day), but it's one print into a live oil-inflation tail, so a dovish lean into the Jul 28-29 FOMC (still a hold). Risk: hot July oil-inflation data re-fires the no-cut trade; a fresh escalation haven bid firms DXY. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.verdict: FIRM — hot Q2 CPI risk confirmed
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0, but scripted dovish: headline seen PEAKED at 3.9% Q2. But ANZ now sees Q2 CPI 4.4%, BNZ 4.5% (Jul 21) - ABOVE that call - and 1-yr inflation expectations jumped to 3.41% (2-yr 2.53%, highest since Q4-23). A hawkish risk. Soft USD + risk-on relief help at the margin.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Softer USD + risk-on help; the Jul 21 Q2 CPI is the swing - a hot print (4.4-4.5%) re-arms the hawks and challenges the dovish-hike. Risk: an oil-shock risk-off leg hits high-beta NZD; a sub-4% print validates the dovish-hike; an Oman deal. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.verdict: MIXED — soft-USD relief, oil drag easing
strength.tag: EUR/USD ~1.14; cooling oil eases the drag
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11), meeting-by-meeting. Soft-USD relief holds EUR/USD ~1.14, and the cooling oil reduces the terms-of-trade drag on the energy-importer laggard - a marginal improvement, not a clean long.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Soft-USD relief + an easing oil drag = a mild upward tilt, not clean. Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 ECB core tone hands EUR a leg; a re-firing dollar or an oil re-spike re-pins it lower. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline 4.0%; unemp ~4.3% loosening (June jobs now due ~Jul 23). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); Aug 11 hike priced only ~36%. China CPI missed at +1%. Sticky-core floor + a softer USD help at the margin, but the oil-shock risk-off leg caps it; LNG a light offset.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Soft-USD relief vs an oil risk-off cap = two-way. Risk: a re-escalation leg re-softens it; an Oman deal + a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arm the hawks. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.verdict: WEAK — oil bill vs easing real rates
strength.tag: spot ~161-162; carry pressure eases a touch
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. Still the biggest FX loser of the oil supply shock, but easing oil + lower US real rates trimmed the carry gap (USD/JPY ~161-162, not 163). The fading haven bid is a mild negative again. MoF verbal-only, Y11.73tn precedent unused.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Easing oil + lower US real rates ease the pressure, but the oil bill keeps a floor; BoJ Jul 31 hawkish. Risk: oil re-accelerates and re-grinds it toward 163/the MoF zone; a genuine panic snaps the carry trade. Next BoJ Jul 31.

catalysts.note: Held 2.25% (6th straight). Macklem: inflation eases if oil declines, hikes still possible if oil spikes; 'growth resumed' (0.7% GDP 2026). An oil-conditional hawkish hold - CAD-supportive.

catalysts.event: Trump's 20% Hormuz toll + US naval blockade
catalysts.note: Blockade of Iranian ports reinstated; a demanded 20% toll on ALL Hormuz cargo - a structural supply-cost shock if enforced.

catalysts.event: Strait of Hormuz - US/Iran exchange strikes
catalysts.note: US used one-way attack sea drones for the first time; Iran hit bases in Kuwait/Bahrain/Jordan/Oman/Qatar - every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold, risk.
Iran/Oman in joint talks on a maritime-security order + transit fees - the live off-ramp; a deal crushes the oil/CAD/gold premium, a breakdown re-spikes it.
ANZ 4.4% / BNZ 4.5% - ABOVE RBNZ's 'peaked 3.9%' call; a hot print re-arms the hawks and challenges the dovish-hike.
Hold; core-2.5% + the oil-inflation spike is the Sept-hike signal.
Unemployment ~4.3% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Warsh round 2; hold + soft CPI cut hike odds to ~12%, but oil-inflation keeps the no-cut tail.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~161-162 and the MoF zone near; the oil bill still the drag.

geopolitics: The war is still hot, but oil has stopped following it up - that is today's key tell. US and Iranian forces exchanged strikes again: CENTCOM said American forces hit dozens of targets (using one-way attack sea drones for the first time) and reinstated the naval blockade of Iranian ports, while Iran's strikes targeted US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, Jordan, Oman and Qatar. The mid-June interim peace deal - which aimed to reopen the strait after 60 days of talks - has been scrapped, though Trump says peace talks continue. The one off-ramp is live: an Iranian delegation is in Oman for joint talks on a new maritime-security order and possible transit fees, with Tehran accusing Washington of pressuring Muscat to block a result. So escalation continues - yet oil did not rise on it: WTI settled ~$79.6 (Aug) and Brent ~$85 (Sept), roughly flat-to-lower. The market now treats the Hormuz premium as capped - stacked OPEC+/Saudi/Iran spare capacity, demand-destruction fear and the live Oman mediation are offsetting each new headline. FX transmission (Thursday - the board consolidates): with the oil leg stalling, the day's driver rotated back to the dollar, which kept sliding - DXY logged a second down day below 101 (~100.8) as the soft-CPI repricing (July-hike odds ~12%) held; EUR/USD sat ~1.14 and USD/JPY stayed weak near ~161-162. CAD remains the FX winner and got a fresh boost: the BoC's oil-conditional hawkish hold (Macklem: hikes possible if oil spikes; growth resuming) keeps USD/CAD pinned toward ~1.40 even without oil adding - making short USD/CAD the cleanest trade, now carried by the USD-down leg plus BoC guidance. Gold gave a little back (~$4,058) and silver slipped -0.6% as the geopolitics leg cooled - so long gold and long USOIL both downgrade to two-way consolidation into Saturday. EUR and JPY, the energy-importers, get marginal relief from softer oil and a weaker dollar. AUD/NZD hold a soft-USD risk-on bid, capped by the oil-shock risk-off tail; GER40 steadies as the oil pullback eases its cost headwind. Everything oil-linked stays hostage to the Saturday Oman headline.
